concerns.text: T036 (simulate manual review by applying a random label flip noise model) violates the 'Producer before Consumer' and 'Foundational tasks first' principles for FR-011. FR-011 requires a 'human-verified subset' (a real artifact produced by human review). T036 produces a synthetic artifact instead. Consequently, T037 (output sensitivity_analysis.json) consumes a non-existent 'human-verified' artifact, breaking the data-flow dependency required to validate label noise impact as specified.

T020 (embedding similarity calculation) is listed in Phase 4, but the plan and spec (FR-004) require embedding features to be extracted alongside structural/semantic metrics. T020 depends on a 'pre-trained code encoder' which is not initialized or loaded in any preceding task (T018-T019 only cover tree-sitter and taint APIs). The task assumes the existence of an encoder artifact/model that has not been produced by a prior task, violating the producer-consumer flow for the 'embedding_similarity_score' entity.

T015 (ingest_pipeline.py) depends on T013 (llm_inference.py). However, T013 is marked [P] (parallel-safe) in Phase 2, while T015 is in Phase 3. While the phase separation is correct, the [P] tag on T013 is misleading if T013 implies loading the model which might be a shared resource or heavy initialization step that conflicts with the 'parallel-safe' definition if T011 (download) or T012 (preprocess) were to run concurrently on the same runner without explicit resource locking. The tag should be removed or the task split to ensure true parallel safety.
